diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers assigned the same frozen unsupported label and cited no evidence, so there is no citation difference. The difference is in how they coded the lack of support: option_1 uses the broader reason code 'insufficient_evidence,' while option_2 uses the more claim-focused 'central_proposition_unsupported.' Given the claim 'apply_index_change_directly' and evidence that discusses read improvement, write regression, tradeoff, and write load, both rationales diagnose the same problem: none of the evidence directly supports the proposition to apply the index change directly. This is best characterized as a granularity difference in reason coding rather than a substantive disagreement. Both confidence ratings are high, so there is no confidence difference.
